source: KMP migration fake-transport planning from Android and iOS file utility error tests

expected.migrationRequirement: Before moving file utility orchestration into common KMP code, implement fake PFTP request and write-stream tests that cover malformed directory payloads, request-level transport errors, response-error status mapping, and write-stream failures after the PUT header is prepared.
observations: captured command and path for every attempted operation
captured PUT payload before write-stream failure mapping
public Android and iOS facade error shape after shared runtime mapping
expected.commonDecision: Preserve documented platform-specific policies only where required for public compatibility; otherwise normalize shared runtime errors to typed request, parse, response, and write-stream failures.

platformExpectations.android: planned facade-level fake-transport characterization for public error mapping
platformExpectations.ios: planned facade-level fake-transport characterization for public error mapping
platformExpectations.common: executable shared commonTest covers file command capture and typed runtime errors before facade mapping moves

execution.reason: Shared commonTest now asserts command capture, malformed directory payload handling, directory-missing status 103, request transport errors, response errors, and write-stream failures through the common fake transport; platform facade tests still need public error mapping before production file orchestration delegates to shared code.

notes: This vector complements executable file read/write/delete and directory-listing vectors with shared commonTest runtime checks. Public Android and iOS facade error mapping remains a migration gate before production file orchestration delegates to shared code.
